# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3'  # specify the version of docker compose file format (could be "2" or higher)
services:   # define all your services here, they should follow this pattern to work properly with other files in a same directory.
    elk_stack:     
        image: elasticsearch:7.8.1  # use the official Elastic search Docker Image version (version can be updated as per requirement)
        
       # Expose ports for access, you might need to adjust these according your needs or setup changes in Kibana and Logstash configurations   
        expose:  
            - "9200" 
          
          # Ports that Elasticsearch will use. Adjust as per requirement if necessary (default is [5671, 5672]). This should match your setup in Kibana and Logstash configurations   
        ports:  
            - "9300"    
        
       # Expose Ports for visualization. Adjust as per requirement or use any other port if needed (default is 5671)     
          logs_visualizer:          
             expose : ["80"]   
              
        environment:  
            - "discovery.type=single-node" # Disable discovery mode, this will not be used in real scenario but for testing purpose    
        
       logstash:  # define Logstash service here (you can use a Dockerfile to build your own)   
           image : dockerized_logstash   # Use the name of you have defined above or provide an existing one. If not provided, it will be pulled from Docker Hub by default    
            volumes: 
                - ./pipelines:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline      # This is where your log parsing pipelines are stored (you can use a local directory)   
           depends_on :  
               - elk_stack         # Make sure Logstash waits for Elasticsearch to be ready before starting. You should adjust this according with the setup of Kibana and ELK stack in your project    
            environment: 
                - LOGSTASH_INTERVAL=10s    # The log interval time (default is '5m')   This will help Logstash run every x seconds after being started. Adjust as per requirement or setup changes Kibana and ELK stack in your project     
        
       kibana:  # define the service for KIBANA here    You can use a Dockerfile to build this (you need an existing one)   It's not recommended if you do it manually, as there is no such thing like that. Use Elasticsearch image instead and expose ports in order to access with browser    
           image: dockerized_kibana    # You should use the name of your KIBANA service or provide an existing one (if any)   If not provided it will be pulled from Docker Hub by default     
        
        depends_on :  - elk_stack       # Make sure all services are started before starting kibana and logstash. You should adjust this according to the setup of ELK stack in your project    
           expose:   ["5601"]    # Expose port for KIBANA UI (default is 5601)      This will help you access with browser from outside, if necessary change it as per requirement. If not provided then no external connection can be made to this service by default
